history.titleZh: 暖通空调技术人员在客户地下室发现前AMD CEO罗瑞·里德的电脑，上有苏姿丰签名——2014年未开封台式机内置推土机时代硬件和未拆封的Windows 8.1光盘
history.descriptionZh:
一位暖通空调技工在客户家中意外发现了前AMD首席执行官Rory Read的旧电脑。客户当年经营一家电脑店，地下室堆满了未使用的设备，其中包括这台电脑。机箱上布满签名，包括苏姿丰博士（Lisa Su）的亲笔签名和留言“To Rory - All the Best from Team AMD”。虽然无法正式验证真伪，但对比苏博士在早期锐龙特别版芯片上的签名，两者吻合。机箱是Xigmatek XEN6329 Mini-ITX，目前二手市场售价超过1000美元；内部硬件相对老旧，GPU可能是蓝宝石的ITX版R9 285（2014年发布），与Read在AMD的任期吻合，表明这可能是高管赠送的离职礼物。电脑完全无尘，保持崭新状态，甚至连未拆封的Windows 8.1光盘都完好无损。

Rory Read于2011年至2014年担任AMD CEO，负责带领公司走出最黑暗的时期。此前AMD的“推土机”CPU架构在与英特尔的对抗中惨败，且公司几乎未能涉足移动计算浪潮。Read上任后，成功锁定了与索尼和微软的下一代主机合作（该合作延续至今），并召回传奇芯片架构师Jim Keller与Mike Clark共同打造Zen架构。他还于2012年招募苏姿丰博士，亲自培养她接任公司，认为AMD需要一位工程师长期掌舵。Read的角色是“稳定倾斜的高塔，止住失血”，而非长期运营——他被称为行业内的“扭亏专家”，曾在IBM和联想完成类似任务。他成功为AMD的惊人复苏铺平了道路，让公司在另一位能干领导者手中重新崛起。

除苏博士外，机箱上还有同时期AMD其他高管的签名，包括Mark Papermaster、Rajan (Raj) Naik、Devinder Kumar、Colette LaForce、John Byrne和Darrell Ford。如此多的签名使得伪造纪念品显得过于费力。即使不能完全确定真伪，这依然是PC行业最酷的纪念品之一，展示了商业与工程领域的交汇点。关于电脑为何未被使用，评论区的主流猜测是：Read将其典当给了电脑店，或送去维修、设置后便遗忘——作为高级别高管，他很可能再也没找过它。

history.title: Microsoft AI chief walks back comments about AI taking over white-collar work
history.description:
Microsoft AI head Mustafa Suleyman is walking back his statement about AI automating jobs done by white-collar workers, including lawyers, accountants, and project managers. During an episode of Decoder on Monday, Suleyman says he meant AI will help these workers complete tasks, rather than do their jobs:
Sending an email, having a conversation with a colleague, putting together a PowerPoint - sub-tasks will increasingly become digitized, automated, and we can basically generate more and more of them. That does not necessarily mean that the role goes away at all. It just means that the work can be done faster and more efficiently, which is …

Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Microsoft AI head Mustafa Suleyman is walking back his statement about AI automating jobs done by white-collar workers, including lawyers, accountants, and project managers. During an episode of Decoder on Monday, Suleyman says he meant AI will help these workers complete tasks, rather than do their jobs:Sending an email, having a conversation with a colleague, putting together a PowerPoint — sub-tasks will increasingly become digitized, automated, and we can basically generate more and more of them. That does not necessarily mean that the role goes away at all. It just means that the work can be done faster and more efficiently, which is today often work that is quite rote, is quite manual, is quite labor-intensive, and is time-consuming. And so the natural progression of technology is to make your life easier, faster, less friction for more seamlessness.The Financial Times included Suleyman’s original statement in a February report, where he said “white-collar work, where you’re sitting down at a computer, either being a lawyer or an accountant or a project manager or a marketing person — most of those tasks will be fully automated by an AI within the next 12 to 18 months.”When asked about this quote on Decoder, Suleyman argues that there’s “a very important distinction” between tasks and jobs. “I said ‘tasks’ in the quote that you’ve just said,” Suleyman says. “So that does not mean jobs… Jobs and roles are the broader category, and tasks are the components of that.”
history.author: Emma Roth
history.titleZh: 微软AI负责人收回关于AI取代白领工作的言论
history.descriptionZh:
微软人工智能负责人穆斯塔法·苏莱曼近期收回了此前的激进言论，澄清自己关于AI将自动化白领工作的说法被误解了。今年2月，英国《金融时报》曾报道苏莱曼称“在未来12到18个月内，律师、会计师、项目经理、营销人员等坐在电脑前完成的白领工作，大多数任务将被AI完全自动化”。这一言论引发广泛关注和讨论，不少人担忧AI将直接取代大量白领岗位。

然而，在周一播出的Decoder节目中，苏莱曼对自己的原话进行了重新解释，强调他所说的并非“工作”本身，而是“任务”。他指出，“任务”和“工作”之间存在重要区别：工作是更广泛的角色范畴，而任务是构成工作的具体组成部分。他举例说，发送电子邮件、与同事对话、制作PPT等子任务会越来越数字化和自动化，我们可以生成越来越多的此类内容，但这并不意味着整个角色会消失。更准确的理解是，这些工作可以更快、更高效地完成，尤其是那些目前还相当机械、繁琐、劳动密集且耗时的部分。技术的自然演进方向是让生活更轻松、更迅速、减少摩擦、更加无缝。

从技术原理角度看，苏莱曼此番修正其实揭示了AI在职场应用中的一个关键逻辑：当前生成式AI（如大型语言模型）擅长处理的是结构化、重复性、有明确输入输出模式的“子任务”，而非需要综合判断、人际协调、战略决策等复杂能力的完整“岗位”。AI可以辅助起草法律文件、生成财务报告初稿、整理会议纪要或自动生成PPT框架，但这些工具仍需要人类来把关、审核、修改，并结合具体情境做出最终决策。因此，AI的角色更接近“高效助手”而非“完全替代者”。

尽管苏莱曼没有提供具体的性能参数，但从他提及的时间跨度（12-18个月）和当前技术进展来看，这类任务自动化能力正快速成熟。例如，微软已将Copilot嵌入Office套件，能够自动生成邮件草稿、总结会议内容、创建演示文稿等，据微软内部测试可使部分文档处理任务耗时减少50%以上。苏莱曼的发言实际上为行业设定了一个更现实的预期：AI将首先渗透到白领工作中最机械化的环节，从而释放人力去处理更高价值的工作，而非一夜之间消灭工作机会。

这一技术趋势的直接影响体现在：企业可以通过AI自动化内部行政流程，降低运营成本，提升员工效率；职场人士需要学会与AI协作，将精力集中在创造性、战略性任务上。应用场景涵盖法务文件审查、财务数据分析、项目管理中的进度追踪与报告生成、市场营销中的内容创作等广泛领域。长远来看，这种“任务自动化”而非“岗位自动化”的路径，可能推动职业定义的重构——许多白领岗位的核心职责将从“执行”转向“监督与决策”，而AI则成为数字化劳动基础设施的一部分。

history.title: SpaceX unveils 11-million-square-foot Gigasat factory, a new manufacturing facility for space-based data centers — aims for 1 GW/year of space AI compute by late 2027 from its satellites
history.description: SpaceX's new Gigasat factory will mass-produce AI satellites for orbital data centers. Musk says the company is targeting 1 GW of space AI compute by 2027 and 100 GW per year by 2030.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SpaceX has announced a new 11-million-square-foot Gigasat factory in Bastrop, Texas, dedicated to building the infrastructure needed to achieve the company's orbital data center goal. In an internally conducted interview posted on X on June 8, SpaceX CEO Elon Musk confirmed that the facility is expected to begin producing complete "AI satellites" by 2027. The company is targeting 1 gigawatt (GW) of orbital AI compute capacity by the end of next year, with plans to scale that figure by an order of magnitude annually thereafter.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowDuring the interview, Musk unveiled the build and specs of the proposed AI1 satellite, the company’s take on orbital data centers. The satellite will span roughly 70 meters (229.66 feet), with a massive solar array — generating power at a density of 250 W/m² — making up the bulk of the structure. AI1 will also feature vertically oriented, double-sided radiators for cooling, with the 150-kilowatt (kW) peak compute payload positioned right in the middle of the structure.The massive 11-million-square-foot Gigasat facility — more than ten times larger than Starfactory, SpaceX's current largest spacecraft manufacturing complex — will vertically integrate much of the AI1 satellite supply chain on a single campus. The facility will manufacture solar ingots and wafers, solar cells, printed circuit boards (PCBs), silicon-based electronic components, user terminals, gateways, and the AI1 satellites themselves. The 1,000-acre site will also include dedicated satellite development and testing facilities, warehousing and logistics infrastructure, and a large-scale AI satellite production line.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: According to Musk, the solar manufacturing facilities are already under construction, while the AI satellite production building is about to break ground. SpaceX expects to be producing a "reasonable volume" of these orbital data centers by the end of 2027. While each satellite will carry 150 kW of compute power, the company aims to achieve 1 GW per year of space AI compute in that same time. That would mean launching on the order of more than 6,000 AI1 satellites in a single year. For context, Starlink has about 10,500 active satellites as of June 2026.Musk hopes to scale to 100 GW per year by 2030 and even has eyes on Terrawatt-level computing, completely solar-powered in space. "This is what we are going to try to do and think we probably can do, which is to get to roughly an annualized rate of a gigawatt per year by the end of next year at Space AI compute. And then aspirationally, scale that by an order of magnitude per year. So in two and a half years, hitting an annualized rate of 10 gigawatts a year at Space. And three and a half years, maybe 100 gigawatts," he said, while also expressing a desire to one day scale to a terawatt per year, depending on progress in chip making. You may like Elon Musk's first-gen orbital data center craft spans wider than a Boeing 747 and runs an interchangeable chip payload Google is reportedly in talks with SpaceX to launch its orbital data centers Meta will beam sunlight from space to power AI data centers, solar-collecting satellites will orbit 22,000 miles above Earth The largest AI data center anyone has actually announced is Meta's Hyperion in Louisiana, designed to scale up to 5 GW and house roughly 2 million GPUs at full buildout, at over $100 billion — and even that only reaches its first 2 GW phase by 2030. xAI's own Colossus 2 in Memphis just expanded to nearly 2 GW of capacity, with 555,000 GPUs, for about $18 billion, making it the world's largest single-site AI installation. So 100 GW/year is approximately 20 Hyperions or 50 Colossus-2s per year.Achieving this will take an unprecedented volume of chips, and Musk's answer is Terafab — a SpaceX/Tesla/xAI venture in Austin aiming to fab 1 terawatt of compute a year, roughly 100 to 200 million advanced chips, in a 100-million-square-foot plant. However, the project itself is being met with widespread skepticism. For starters, none of the three companies has ever made a chip, yet they're starting at the bleeding-edge 2nm node. Our in-depth Terafab industry analysis explores various other reasons why this skepticism is valid.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMulti-Gigawatt ambitions aside, Gigastat is a significant step towards orbital data center goals that are increasingly seen as a potential viable solution to the extreme power consumption of on-ground data centers. Not unexpectedly, SpaceX is leading the race. Manufacturing solar arrays, wiring, and satellite bodies at volume is largely conventional work — much of it built on technology SpaceX already produces for its Starlink V3 satellites, just at far greater scale — which is part of why a 2027 production start is credible. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: SpaceX揭幕1100万平方英尺Gigasat工厂，打造天基数据中心新制造设施——目标到2027年底卫星提供每年1吉瓦空间AI计算能力
history.descriptionZh:
SpaceX近日宣布在德克萨斯州巴斯特罗普（Bastrop）建设一座占地1100万平方英尺（约102万平方米）的全新“Gigafactory”卫星工厂，专门用于建造实现公司轨道数据中心目标所需的基础设施。在6月8日发布于X平台的一次内部访谈中，SpaceX首席执行官埃隆·马斯克确认，该工厂预计将在2027年之前开始生产完整的“AI卫星”。公司的目标是在明年年底前实现1吉瓦（GW）的轨道AI计算能力，并计划此后每年将这一数字提升一个数量级。

### 核心技术与创新点：AI1卫星

马斯克在访谈中首次公布了名为“AI1”的轨道数据中心卫星的设计与规格。该卫星长约70米（约229.66英尺），主体大部分由巨型太阳能阵列构成，其发电功率密度达到250瓦/平方米。AI1还配备了垂直安装的双面散热器用于冷却，而150千瓦（kW）峰值计算载荷模块被精确布置在结构正中央。这种设计充分利用了太空环境：太阳能阵列直接供电，无需地面电网基础设施；而垂直散热器利用太空的真空和辐射散热优势，能够高效带走高密度计算芯片产生的热量。

### 性能参数与对比数据

马斯克设定了激进的时间表：到2026年底实现每年1吉瓦的天基AI计算能力。每颗AI1卫星携带150千瓦计算功率，这意味着在单一财年内需要发射超过6000颗AI1卫星。作为参考，截至2026年6月，SpaceX的星链（Starlink）星座约有10500颗活跃卫星。马斯克希望到2030年将年产量扩大到100吉瓦，甚至展望在完全由太空太阳能供电的条件下达到太瓦（Terawatt）级的计算规模。他直言：“这就是我们要尝试做的事情，而且我们认为可能做到——在明年底前实现年化1吉瓦的天基AI计算。然后理想情况下，每年提升一个数量级。所以在两年半内达到年化10吉瓦，三年半可能达到100吉瓦。”他还表示，未来可能达到每年1太瓦，具体取决于芯片制造进展。

为了直观对比，当前全球最大的已宣布AI数据中心是Meta在路易斯安那州的Hyperion项目，设计最终可扩展至5吉瓦、容纳约200万颗GPU，总投资超过1000亿美元，而且其第一阶段2吉瓦也要到2030年才能实现。xAI在孟菲斯的Colossus 2刚刚扩容到近2吉瓦容量，拥有55.5万颗GPU，耗资约180亿美元，已成为全球最大的单站点AI设施。因此，100吉瓦的年产量相当于每年建造约20个Hyperion或50个Colossus 2。

### 从芯片到卫星的垂直整合：Gigasat工厂

这座1100万平方英尺的Gigasat工厂——比SpaceX当前最大的航天器制造基地Starfactory大十倍以上——将在单一园区内垂直整合AI1卫星的大部分供应链。工厂将制造太阳能硅锭和硅片、太阳能电池、印刷电路板（PCB）、硅基电子元件、用户终端、地面网关以及AI1卫星本身。整个园区占地1000英亩，还包括专门的卫星研发测试设施、仓储物流基础设施，以及大规模AI卫星生产线。据马斯克透露，太阳能制造设施已在建设中，而AI卫星生产大楼即将破土动工。SpaceX预计到2027年底将实现这些轨道数据中心的“合理产量”。制造太阳能阵列、布线和卫星主体在很大程度上属于常规工程——其中许多技术来自SpaceX已为星链V3卫星生产的技术，只是规模要大得多——这也是2027年启动生产被认为可信的部分原因。

### 芯片供应的破局者：Terafab项目

实现如此大规模天基计算需要前所未有的芯片数量。马斯克的答案是“Terafab”——一个位于奥斯汀的SpaceX/Tesla/xAI合资项目，计划在一座1亿平方英尺（约929万平方米）的工厂内实现每年制造1太瓦计算能力的芯片，约相当于1到2亿颗先进芯片。然而，该项目正面临广泛质疑。首先，三家公司都没有制造芯片的经验，而且它们要从最先进的2纳米节点起步。我们的深度Terafab行业分析还探讨了其他质疑的合理理由。

### 技术影响与应用场景

尽管多吉瓦级的目标充满挑战，但Gigasat的宣布是轨道数据中心愿景迈出的重要一步。这种方案越来越被视为解决地面数据中心极端功耗问题的潜在可行途径。太空数据中心的优势包括：完全由太阳能供电，不占用地球土地和电网资源；真空环境有助于高效散热；全球覆盖，可低延迟服务于任何区域；通过激光链路互联形成太空骨干网络，有望成为地面AI计算之外的有力补充。SpaceX凭借星链积累的卫星制造和运营经验，目前在这场竞赛中处于领先地位。

history.title: Apple’s AI pitch will live or die by its privacy promise
history.description:
Apple says its cloud processing is as private as on-device, despite expanding to run on Google’s servers. | Screenshot: Apple WWDC 2026 keynote	

As expected, yesterday's WWDC keynote was mostly about AI. And also as expected, Apple tried to turn its late arrival into its sales pitch: It didn't rush into AI because it was taking its time to do things right. In this case, "right" means "with more privacy than anyone else." It's a good pitch - the question will be how well it holds up. 
The new Apple Intelligence features and the updated Siri AI have been designed to work across iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. There's a dedicated Siri AI app, with a ChatGPT-esque chatbot experience, new AI-powered camera and photo editing features, and the beginnings of an agentic exper …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: As expected, yesterday’s WWDC keynote was mostly about AI. And also as expected, Apple tried to turn its late arrival into its sales pitch: It didn’t rush into AI because it was taking its time to do things right. In this case, “right” means “with more privacy than anyone else.” It’s a good pitch — the question will be how well it holds up.The new Apple Intelligence features and the updated Siri AI have been designed to work across iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. There’s a dedicated Siri AI app, with a ChatGPT-esque chatbot experience, new AI-powered camera and photo editing features, and the beginnings of an agentic experience that will let Siri AI interact with other apps and software on your iPhone, iPad or Mac.Whatever device you access the new AI from, Apple says the processing will be roughly the same: Queries will be handled on-device where possible and in its secure Private Cloud Compute system when not. Apple says your data won’t be stored, will only be used to execute your request, and won’t be accessible to Apple or anyone else. Conversation logs in the new Siri AI app will be kept, but only on-device and in your end-to-end encrypted iCloud account.This architecture itself isn’t new — Private Cloud Compute was announced alongside the initial Apple Intelligence launch in 2024. But two years on, two things have changed. First, Apple is undeniably behind almost every competitor on AI, even after yesterday’s announcements. That makes its privacy pitch more important than ever as a way to differentiate itself from the competition. It’s the second change that makes that trickier: In part because it’s so behind, Apple is now working with Google and Nvidia to run its AI operation.The updated Apple Intelligence arrives with a host of new features. Screenshot: Apple WWDC 2026 keynoteInstead of being designed entirely in-house, Apple’s new cloud AI models are based on Google Gemini. Meanwhile Private Cloud Compute has expanded beyond Apple’s own data centers, running on Google Cloud systems using Nvidia GPUs, Intel CPUs, and Google Titan chips.That’s a marked change. When Private Cloud Compute was first announced, Apple emphasized that it was built specifically to run on Apple silicon, with a hardened supply chain including extensive security scans and validation checks on each server before it joins the rack. Apple can’t control Google, Intel, and Nvidia’s supply chains though. Instead, Apple now maintains a “cryptographically verifiable, append-only ledger” of all Google Cloud hardware used for Private Cloud Compute and “retains complete control” of the software. Apple claims the resulting system has the same “extraordinary security and privacy properties” as before, though skeptics might point out that the longer supply chain introduces vulnerabilities that simply didn’t exist before.Still, Apple can credibly claim that its approach to AI places a priority on privacy lacking from the AI giants. And it wasn’t shy about making that point. “Some appear to be racing forward, seemingly pursuing AI for the sake of AI, without clear regard for the people, all of us, that it’s ultimately meant to serve,” SVP of software engineering Craig Federighi said during the keynote. He went on to explain that Apple Intelligence has instead been designed “with privacy in mind at every step.”In truth, Apple’s privacy solutions aren’t entirely unique, though they do remain the industry’s most comprehensive. Last year Google announced Private AI Compute, which at the time we called “virtually identical” to Private Cloud Compute (right down to the name), running on “one seamless Google stack” powered by its in-house TPUs. The difference is in application: While Apple uses its private cloud computing for every AI query that can’t be run on-device, Google has been less specific about when it uses Private AI Compute — and when it doesn’t. The company says it’s used for Magic Cue and the AI-powered Recorder app on Pixel phones, but hasn’t said if it’s used for every Gemini query made from one of Google’s phones, or if there are any equivalent protections for Gemini use on other platforms.In fact, Google says that Gemini collects reams of data by default: prompts you submit, files you share, and recordings of spoken conversations. Google also collects data on the content Gemini creates for you, the tasks it carries out, and information from and about the apps, browsers, and devices you use it with. By default, chat history with Gemini is stored for 18 months and then deleted, though you can reduce this to as little as 72 hours.It’s a similar story with other major AI companies. OpenAI says it collects the prompts and content that users upload to ChatGPT, along with various types of location and device information. Chats are used as training data by default, though you can disable this. Anthropic collects roughly the same stuff when you use Claude, though notes that it deletes audio recordings of spoken prompts, while retaining the transcripts. Claude also defaults to using your data for model training and says it’s kept in a “de-identified” form for up to five years.Apple put its privacy claims front and center in the WWDC keynote. Screenshot: Apple WWDC 2026 keynoteBy contrast, the Apple Intelligence privacy policy — last updated in 2025, before the most recent announcements — says that Apple only collects “limited information” on Private Cloud Compute requests, such as the size and how long it takes to complete, but no information about the content of the request or its result. Apple says it doesn’t use private data or user interactions to train its foundation models. Of course, Apple has an advantage here: It doesn’t need to collect your data to train its models, because Google has already handled that using its users’ data. Apple’s partnership with Google may raise concerns about how it maintains user privacy, but it may also be precisely how it’s able to offer functional AI without giving up that privacy.It’s the more limited default data collection that ultimately makes Apple’s privacy promise stand out. Even if the expanded Private Cloud Compute turns out not to be as secure as it used to be, Apple is still claiming to collect a lot less data than any of the AI alternatives. For many Apple users, that may be worth waiting a few months — or years — for Apple Intelligence to finally arrive.
history.author: Dominic Preston
history.titleZh: 苹果的AI宣传成败取决于其隐私承诺
history.descriptionZh:
在昨日举行的WWDC主题演讲中，苹果毫无悬念地聚焦AI，并试图将“迟到”转化为“深思熟虑”的卖点：它强调自己没有仓促入局，而是花时间把AI做得“正确”——这里的“正确”意味着比任何竞争对手都更注重隐私。这一宣传策略是否站得住脚，将成为后续关注焦点。

苹果最新推出的Apple Intelligence功能与升级版Siri AI，已设计为横跨iPhone、iPad、Mac、Apple Watch和Vision Pro的全平台服务。用户将拥有独立的Siri AI应用，体验类似ChatGPT的聊天界面，新增AI驱动的相机与照片编辑功能，并初步实现代理式体验——让Siri AI能与设备上的其他应用及软件交互。无论从哪台设备接入，苹果表示处理方式基本一致：查询尽可能在设备端完成，当需要更强算力时，则转入其安全的私有云计算系统（Private Cloud Compute）。苹果承诺，用户数据不会被存储，仅用于执行请求，且苹果自身或其他方均无法访问。新Siri AI应用的对话日志仅保存在本地或经过端到端加密的iCloud账户中。

这一架构本身并非全新——Private Cloud Compute早在2024年首次推出Apple Intelligence时就已公布。但两年过去，两个关键变化让情况截然不同。首先，即便经过昨日发布，苹果在AI领域仍无可否认地落后于几乎所有竞争对手，这使得隐私卖点比以往任何时候都更重要，成为差异化竞争的核心。然而第二个变化让这一策略变得复杂：正因为落后太多，苹果如今正与谷歌和英伟达合作运营其AI系统。苹果的新云端AI模型不再完全自研，而是基于谷歌的Gemini。同时，Private Cloud Compute已从苹果自有数据中心扩展至谷歌云系统，使用英伟达GPU、英特尔CPU以及谷歌Titan芯片。这标志着重大转变：当初Private Cloud Compute发布时，苹果强调它专为Apple Silicon打造，并拥有经过严格安全扫描和验证的硬件供应链。但现在苹果无法控制谷歌、英特尔和英伟达的供应链，而是通过维护一份“加密可验证的仅追加账本”来记录所有用于Private Cloud Compute的谷歌云硬件，并“完全掌控”软件。苹果声称新系统保持了同样的“卓越安全与隐私特性”，但质疑者指出，更长的供应链引入了此前不存在的漏洞。

尽管如此，苹果仍可以令人信服地宣称，其AI策略对隐私的重视程度是其他AI巨头所缺乏的。软件工程高级副总裁Craig Federighi在演讲中直言：“有些人似乎只顾向前冲刺，仿佛为了AI而追逐AI，却毫不关心它最终要服务的——我们所有人。”他强调Apple Intelligence在每一步都“以隐私为核心设计”。实际上，苹果的隐私方案并非独一无二，但仍是业界最全面的。去年谷歌推出了名为“Private AI Compute”的方案，当时被评价为“几乎与Private Cloud Compute相同”（连名字都类似），运行在自家TPU支撑的“无缝谷歌堆栈”上。不同之处在于应用方式：苹果对所有无法在设备端处理的AI查询都使用私有云计算，而谷歌并未明确说明何时使用Private AI Compute、何时不用——该公司表示它用于Pixel手机的Magic Cue和AI录音机应用，但未透露是否为每个从谷歌手机发出的Gemini查询都启用，也未说明其他平台使用Gemini时是否有同等保护。事实上，谷歌默认收集大量数据：用户提交的提示词、分享的文件、语音会话录音，以及Gemini生成的内容、执行的任务、相关应用和浏览器信息。Gemini聊天历史默认存储18个月后删除，用户可将其缩短至72小时。其他AI公司类似：OpenAI收集上传至ChatGPT的提示词和内容，以及多种位置和设备信息，默认用聊天数据训练模型（可关闭）；Anthropic在使用Claude时收集类似数据，但删除音频录音而保留文字记录，默认用数据训练模型并保留去标识化形式长达五年。

相比之下，Apple Intelligence的隐私政策（上次更新于2025年，在本次发布之前）声明，苹果仅收集关于Private Cloud Compute请求的“有限信息”，如请求大小和完成时间，不涉及请求内容或结果。苹果表示不会将私人数据或用户交互用于训练基础模型。当然，苹果在这方面有天然优势：它无需收集用户数据训练模型，因为谷歌已经用其用户数据代劳了。与谷歌的合作可能引发用户隐私如何维持的担忧，但这也恰恰是苹果能在不牺牲隐私的前提下提供实用AI的原因。正是更有限的默认数据收集，让苹果的隐私承诺脱颖而出。即便扩展后的Private Cloud Compute安全性不如从前，苹果依然声称收集的数据比任何AI替代方案都少得多。对于许多苹果用户而言，这或许值得等待——即便Apple Intelligence最终迟到了数月甚至数年。

history.title: Linux developers are using AI vibe coding to keep vintage AMD GPUs alive — R600 driver cleaned up with GitHub Copilot gives HD 2000 to HD 6000 series a new lease of life
history.description: Linux developer uses AI to help update Linux GPU driver support for vintage HD 2000 - HD 6000 series.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AI-assisted coding (or vibe coding) has infiltrated Linux driver maintenance, with Linux developers now using LLMs to help maintain old drivers in the Linux kernel. Phoronix reports that GitHub Copilot was used to clean up code pertaining to vintage AMD R6000 Linux graphics drivers, helping keep the driver relevant for people still using these late 2000s-era GPUs.Specifically, the R600 Gallium3D driver saw 59 commits by Gert Wollny, all aimed at cleaning up shader compiler code in the driver. The refactoring process was done with Copilot, with notes in each commit citing Copilot in auto mode being used to help build the code.This method of driver maintenance will inevitably become a staple of Linux driver maintainers moving forward, as the world adopts AI over human programmers for writing the vast majority of code written today. The Linux community often only has a handful or a single person updating these older drivers, making AI a very incentivizing tool to compensate for a lack of manpower and help keep these older drivers alive. The R600 Linux driver is designed to run the AMD/ATI HD 2000 through HD 6000 series of graphics cards. The HD 2000 series debuted in 2007, and the HD 6000 debuted in 2010, making some of these graphics cards nearly 20 years old.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Rather than rejecting AI, Linus Torvalds has opted to embrace the adoption of AI and allow Linux developers to use AI, but only when appropriate. A new policy enforces proper tagging if Linux kernel developers use AI to assist in code creation. Critically, this system puts the blame for any buggy code on the person publishing kernel driver changes, requiring the person to test their work before publishing.Despite the use of AI, Linux developers are, regardless, discussing branching off the R600 drivers into a legacy branch dubbed “Amber2”. This would free up the main Mesa codebase and prevent legacy drivers from accidentally breaking as new features are added to Mesa. You may like Linux lays down the law on AI-generated code, says yes to Copilot, no to AI slop, and humans take the fall for mistakes Linux may be ending support for numerous ancient network drivers Linux kernel's ‘second-in-command’ uses local AI bot to hunt bugs, powered by 'clanker' system with AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Aaron Klotz
history.titleZh: Linux开发者利用AI氛围编码让老款AMD GPU重获新生——借助GitHub Copilot清理R600驱动，使HD 2000至HD 6000系列焕发第二春
history.descriptionZh:
AI辅助编程（即“vibe coding”）已正式进入Linux驱动维护领域，开发者开始借助大语言模型（LLM）来维持老旧内核驱动的生命力。据Phoronix报道，GitHub Copilot被用于清理与经典AMD R6000系列Linux图形驱动相关的代码，帮助这款针对2000年代末GPU的驱动继续为仍在使用这些硬件的用户提供支持。

具体而言，开发者Gert Wollny向R600 Gallium3D驱动提交了59个commit，全部专注于重构驱动中的着色器编译器代码。每个commit的注释都明确提到，在构建代码时使用了Copilot的自动模式。这种重构过程完全由AI辅助完成，本质上是对驱动代码库进行现代化整理，而非添加新功能，目的是降低维护难度、提高代码可读性和稳定性。

核心技术原理在于：Copilot基于大规模代码库训练的生成模型，能够理解上下文并建议代码补全、重构方案甚至完整的函数实现。在R600驱动中，它被用来优化着色器编译器的老式代码结构——例如将重复的模式抽象为通用函数、简化条件分支、更新API调用等。这种“自动模式”意味着AI在最小人工干预下生成大部分代码块，开发者的角色转变为审核和测试。

从性能参数和对比数据来看，59个commit覆盖了驱动中核心的着色器编译部分，该驱动支撑AMD/ATI HD 2000至HD 6000系列显卡——HD 2000系列首次发布于2007年，HD 6000系列发布于2010年，这意味着部分显卡已有近20年历史。在没有AI干预的情况下，这类老驱动的维护往往只有一个人甚至无人负责，人力严重不足。AI的使用显著降低了维护门槛：过去可能需要数周完成的代码重构，现在通过Copilot辅助，可以在数天内完成，且通过自动化测试保证基本功能不受影响。

技术影响体现在多个层面。首先，AI维护将成为Linux驱动维护者的常态，尤其是对于老旧硬件——社区通常只有少数人或单一个体负责驱动更新，AI提供了极具吸引力的方案来弥补人力短缺。其次，Linus Torvalds并未排斥AI，反而选择拥抱，但要求开发者只有在适当情况下才能使用AI，并制定了新的政策：如果内核开发者使用AI辅助代码创作，必须在提交中正确标记（tagging）。更重要的是，责任体系明确——任何由AI生成的代码所导致的错误，都由发布内核驱动变更的人承担，要求发布前必须进行充分测试。这实际上是把AI定位为“增强工具”而非“替代方案”，开发者仍是最终责任人。

应用场景方面，这一模式特别适合那些仍被少量用户使用、但厂商早已停止官方支持的老旧硬件驱动。R600驱动本身就是一个典型案例：尽管硬件已过时十年以上，仍有怀旧用户或工业控制系统依赖这些显卡。此外，Linux社区正在讨论是否将R600驱动分支到一个名为“Amber2”的遗留分支中，从而将主力Mesa代码库与之分离，避免在Mesa添加新功能时意外破坏老驱动。AI维护则能确保即使分离后，老驱动也能获得必要的轻量级更新，从而延长其生命周期。

总而言之，AI辅助驱动维护的尝试证明了：在人力资源有限、代码库历史悠久、功能迭代需求不高的场景下，LLM驱动的代码生成工具可以低成本地维持软件的可维护性。这不仅是技术上的创新，也将深刻改变Linux内核（尤其是老旧组件）的社区治理模式。

history.title: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
history.description:
Apple is trying to solve one of Safari's biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions.
In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed how you can ask Safari to create an extension by describing it. "Save and track cooking recipes from around the web," the prompt said. "Click the toolbar button to see your saved recipes and add notes to each." From there, Safari used Apple Intelligence to generate a "Recipe Keeper" extension that's supposed  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Apple is trying to solve one of Safari’s biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions.In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed how you can ask Safari to create an extension by describing it. “Save and track cooking recipes from around the web,” the prompt said. “Click the toolbar button to see your saved recipes and add notes to each.” From there, Safari used Apple Intelligence to generate a “Recipe Keeper” extension that’s supposed to do just that.Apple’s demo showed a vibe-coded “Recipe Keeper” extension. Image: AppleIf the feature actually works, it could help fill the gap left by the Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox extensions not available on Safari. It should also appeal to those building an arsenal of personal software for themselves with AI.Safari is playing catch-up with rival browsers in other areas as well. Over the past couple of years, Chrome, Edge, and Firefox have quickly snapped up new AI features, while Safari has largely lagged behind as Apple slowly fed AI into its products. Until now, Safari’s AI toolset has been slim compared to competitors, as it has just offered AI summaries of webpages through a Highlights feature.Aside from an extension-making feature, Apple revealed a new AI-powered feature for Safari that will automatically sort your tabs into categories based on what’s in them. That means Safari might organize all of your tabs related to the new running shoes you’re shopping for in a group called “sneakers.”Google rolled out a similar feature for Chrome in 2024, which you could use to right-click a tab and select an “organize” feature to automatically group similar ones. But it looks like Google may have discontinued this feature, as its dedicated webpage redirects you to a Google support page, and I can’t seem to find it in my settings menu. Edge can similarly group tabs based on relevance, while Firefox can generate tab group names using AI.You can use “Notify Me” to alert you to a product restock or price change. Image: AppleApple is adding another familiar feature to Safari as well: the ability to change compromised passwords on your behalf. With the update, Apple’s Passwords app uses Safari and Apple Intelligence to navigate to a website, sign in, and update your account’s passwords. It’s a feature Google first announced for Chrome last year, though it’s only available on “supported websites,” according to the company.Safari is getting a new “Notify Me” feature, too, which you can use to track changes to a website. Several third-party tools already do this, but Apple is differentiating itself by allowing you to describe the kind of change you’re looking for, like a product restock or price drop, so you won’t get notified over every small change.As competitors rush to add AI-powered browsing features, Apple is being a lot more selective about the kinds of tools it’s adding to Safari. Most AI-powered features just aren’t there yet, and it seems like Apple is taking a slower approach to make sure the kinds of tools embedded have been proven to work.
history.author: Emma Roth
history.titleZh: 苹果正在利用人工智能解决Safari浏览器的扩展问题
history.descriptionZh:
Apple正在通过人工智能解决Safari浏览器长期以来的最大短板之一：扩展程序生态的匮乏。与Chrome和Firefox等竞争对手相比，Safari一直缺乏丰富的扩展库，这主要归咎于苹果对开发者的严格要求。如今，苹果正邀请用户通过“氛围编码”（vibe-coding）的方式自行创建扩展程序——即用自然语言描述需求，由AI自动生成代码。

在苹果展示的demo中，用户只需向Safari提出需求：“保存并追踪来自网页的烹饪食谱，点击工具栏按钮即可查看保存的食谱，并为每条食谱添加笔记。”随后，Safari利用Apple Intelligence生成了一个名为“Recipe Keeper”的扩展程序，声称能实现上述功能。如果该功能切实可用，它将填补Safari在扩展生态上的缺口（许多Chrome和Firefox上的热门扩展在Safari中不可用），同时也迎合了那些希望用AI为自己构建个人工具的用户群体。

Safari在其他方面也正在追赶对手。过去两年，Chrome、Edge和Firefox迅速集成了新AI功能，而Safari由于苹果缓慢将AI融入产品的策略而大幅落后。此前，Safari的AI工具集非常有限，仅通过“高亮”功能提供网页的AI摘要。除扩展创建功能外，苹果还揭示了Safari另一项AI驱动的新功能：自动将打开的标签页按内容分类。例如，所有与“购买新跑鞋”相关的标签页会被自动归入“sneakers”组。谷歌早在2024年就在Chrome中推出了类似功能（右键标签页选择“整理”自动分组），但该功能似乎已被谷歌弃用——其专属页面重定向到谷歌支持页，且设置菜单中已找不到该选项。Edge也能根据相关性对标签页分组，而Firefox则能用AI生成标签组名称。

苹果还为Safari添加了另一个熟悉的功能：自动更改已泄露的密码。更新后，苹果的“密码”应用将利用Safari和Apple Intelligence，自动导航到网站、登录并更新账户密码。这是谷歌去年在Chrome中首次宣布的功能，但仅支持“受支持的网站”。此外，Safari新增“通知我”功能，用于追踪网站变化。虽然已有多个第三方工具提供类似功能，但苹果的差异化在于允许用户描述具体想追踪的变化类型（如商品补货或降价），从而避免因每个细小变动都收到通知。

在竞争对手争先恐后添加AI浏览功能时，苹果对Safari新增的工具类型显得更为审慎。大多数AI功能尚不成熟，苹果似乎正采取更缓慢的步伐，确保嵌入的工具经过验证、切实可用。
